extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by versus against each party. I counted only the “Against” rows: criminal proceedings are 0 against the Company, 1 against Directors and 1 against Promoters, totaling 2; statutory/regulatory proceedings are 1 against the Company and none against subsidiaries, totaling 1; tax proceedings against the Company and its Subsidiaries are 41 against the Company and nil against Subsidiaries, totaling 41. For the aggregate amount against the Company and Subsidiaries, I used the quantified “Against our Company” amount of ₹2,387.24 million and excluded Director/Promoter matters; this converts to ₹238.724 crore (₹2,387.24 million ÷ 10). The contingent-liabilities table is dated September 30, 2024 and has no stated Total row, so the two disclosed items were summed: ₹259.91 million + ₹36.37 million = ₹296.28 million, or ₹29.628 crore. The concentration figures are company-wide top-five and top-ten customer percentages of revenue from operations for the full Financial Year 2024: 31.17% and 46.26%, respectively. The source monetary tables use ₹ millions; all crore fields were converted using 1 crore = ₹10 million.
